description: Repo-local agent harness with AGENTS.md, README, architecture, testing, quality, feature list, progress log, and active exec plan.
acceptance_steps: AGENTS.md links to architecture, testing, quality, active exec plan, progress log, and feature list.
README mentions every public CLI command and required env var.
Feature list is valid JSON.

description: Reusable Yandex LLM helper compatible with scripts/yandex_llm_example.py.
acceptance_steps: Request body matches Yandex Foundation Models completion shape.
Completion text extraction supports Yandex alternatives payload.

description: Analyze pages into structured opportunity JSON without guessing missing facts.
acceptance_steps: Invalid LLM JSON is rejected.
Missing fields are filled with safe defaults.
VK Dobro-like and SberVmeste-like fixtures are parsed.

description: Local workflow supports add-link, analyze, checklist, draft, and digest.
acceptance_steps: Local JSON integration flow works without external services.
Drafts use only FundWiki and mark missing facts.
Digest sorts overdue and urgent actions deterministically.

description: Telegram command handlers expose digest, add_link, checklist, draft, and status behavior.
acceptance_steps: Handlers return text replies without sending external messages in tests.
Status updates go through the store and activity log.

description: Harness includes the original fundraising-system intent and a staged roadmap for future agents beyond the MVP.
acceptance_steps: docs/ROADMAP.md captures the original task, product north star, MVP, and future agent phases.
AGENTS.md and README.md point future agents to docs/ROADMAP.md.
Domain docs refer to the roadmap instead of duplicating all future scope.

description: Harness requires detailed operator-facing service usage instructions to stay current.
acceptance_steps: docs/USAGE.md explains setup, env vars, local store, basic workflow, discovery, Telegram, Google Sheets, and human review.
AGENTS.md explicitly requires updating docs/USAGE.md when service usage changes.
README.md links to docs/USAGE.md.

description: Harness captures UI direction for a non-IT fundraiser before the MVP grows into a broader system.
acceptance_steps: docs/UI_STRATEGY.md identifies the primary non-IT user and UI principles.
docs/UI_STRATEGY.md outlines the first UI slice and future UI phases.
AGENTS.md, README.md, ROADMAP.md, and QUALITY.md point future agents to the UI strategy.

description: A non-IT operator can run the first verified workflow with a selectable store backend, diagnostics, and offline smoke test.
acceptance_steps: CLI supports --store-backend local|google and doctor.
Local smoke workflow exercises add-link, analyze, checklist, draft, and digest without external services.
README and docs/USAGE.md document doctor, BALANCE_STORE_BACKEND, --store-backend, and operator recipes.
docs/UI_STRATEGY.md ties the first web UI slice to the same operator workflow.

description: Local server-rendered web UI exposes the first non-IT operator workflow without external sends.
acceptance_steps: CLI supports web with --host and --port plus BALANCE_WEB_HOST and BALANCE_WEB_PORT.
Web UI renders dashboard, opportunity list, opportunity detail, checklist, draft, and local analysis form.
POST handlers add links and run heuristic analysis through existing services.
README, docs/USAGE.md, and docs/UI_STRATEGY.md document the local web UI.
scripts/dev_check.py includes a web render smoke check.
